name: DeDe Chapline
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Planetary geology • Hyperspectral imaging • Mineral mapping
bio: Dede Chapline is a graduate research assistant in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University, working within the Planetary Instrumentation eXperimentation and Exploration Laboratory (PIXEL). Her research focuses on planetary geology, surface processes, and the evolution of planetary bodies, particularly through the use of remote sensing techniques and planetary analog studies. She integrates geospatial analysis and spectral data to investigate surface composition and environmental dynamics, with experience contributing to projects involving hyperspectral imaging and mineral mapping. Chapline’s work combines data analysis, remote sensing, and field-based approaches to better understand planetary surfaces and their development over time.
